ucs.title: [LLM] Emissary Panda HyperBro loader: DLL side-loading via signed binary relocated to ProgramData/Public
ucs.description: Hunts TG-3390's signature HyperBro implant deployment, where a legitimately-signed third-party binary (commonly ThinSoft thinmon.exe, vftrace.exe, or Symantec/Trend/Sophos utilities) is dropped together with a malicious side-loaded DLL and an encrypted .dat payload into world-writable directories (ProgramData, Users\Public, AppData). This trio of file co-location plus signed-process executing from a non-install path is the LuckyMouse/BRONZE UNION HyperBro fingerprint.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.process_hash) as process_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("thinmon.exe","vftrace.exe","VFTrace.exe","AppLaunch.exe","hpqhvind.exe","GUP.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe")) AND (Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.dat" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.bin" OR Filesystem.file_name="thinmon.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="hccutils.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="libcurl.dll") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_path as side_loaded_artifact] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - count
ucs.kql:
let suspectDirs = dynamic([@"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\appdata\local\temp\", @"\perflogs\"]);
let hyperbroLoaders = dynamic(["thinmon.exe","vftrace.exe","applaunch.exe","hpqhvind.exe","gup.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe","vssvc.exe"]);
let sideloadDlls = dynamic(["thinmon.dll","hccutils.dll","libcurl.dll","vftrace.dll","msvcr100.dll"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ (hyperbroLoaders)
| where tolower(FolderPath) has_any (suspectDirs)
| extend SignedLegit = (InitiatingProcessSignatureStatus == "Valid" or ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any ("ThinSoft","Symantec","Trend","Hewlett","Google","Microsoft"))
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where tolower(FolderPath) has_any (suspectDirs)
    | where FileName in~ (sideloadDlls) or FileName endswith ".dat" or FileName endswith ".bin"
    | project DeviceId, DroppedFile=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, DropTime=Timestamp, DropInitiator=InitiatingProcessFileName
) on DeviceId
| where DropTime between ((Timestamp - 1h) .. (Timestamp + 1h))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, DroppedFile, DroppedPath, DropInitiator, SHA256
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Generic DLL-sideload hunts return mountains of FPs from legitimate installers. This rule narrows to the exact signed-binary names TG-3390 has reused for 7+ years to load HyperBro (ThinSoft thinmon, VFTrace, AppLaunch), pinned to ProgramData/Public/PerfLogs (their preferred staging dirs), AND requires temporal co-location with a sibling .dat/.bin or known sideload DLL — the three-file HyperBro patt

ucs.title: [LLM] TG-3390 SharePoint/IIS China Chopper webshell pivoting to WinRM lateral movement
ucs.description: Catches Emissary Panda's hallmark intrusion chain: exploitation of internet-facing SharePoint/Exchange/IIS (CVE-2019-0604, ProxyLogon) to drop a China Chopper .aspx webshell, w3wp.exe spawning recon/credential commands, then lateral movement via WinRM (winrs.exe / wsmprovhost.exe) — a sequence repeatedly attributed to APT27/BRONZE UNION/Iron Tiger by SecureWorks and NCC Group.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","ntdsutil.exe","vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe","winrs.exe","reg.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval phase="webshell_recon" | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="wsmprovhost.exe" OR Processes.process_name="winrs.exe" OR (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" AND Processes.process="*Invoke-Command*" AND Processes.process="*-ComputerName*")) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval phase="winrm_lateral"] | stats values(phase) as phases values(cmdline) as cmds count by dest | where mvcount(phases)>=2 | sort - count
ucs.kql:
let webshellRecon = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","ntdsutil.exe","vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe","winrs.exe","reg.exe","hostname.exe")
| project ReconTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ReconAccount=AccountName, ReconCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ReconParentPath=InitiatingProcessFolderPath;
let winrmPivot = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "wsmprovhost.exe")
   or (FileName =~ "winrs.exe")
   or (FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Invoke-Command","Enter-PSSession","New-PSSession") and ProcessCommandLine has "-ComputerName")
| project PivotTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, PivotAccount=AccountName, PivotCmd=ProcessCommandLine, PivotProc=FileName;
let aspxDrop = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\TEMPLATE\LAYOUTS\", @"\Microsoft\Exchange Server\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe")
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ShellPath=FolderPath, ShellName=FileName;
webshellRecon
| join kind=inner winrmPivot on DeviceId
| where PivotTime between ((ReconTime) .. (ReconTime + 6h))
| join kind=leftouter aspxDrop on DeviceId
| project ReconTime, DeviceName, ReconAccount, ReconCmd, PivotTime, PivotProc, PivotCmd, ShellName, ShellPath
| sort by ReconTime desc
ucs.rationale: TG-3390 is one of the few state-aligned crews that consistently chains (1) public-facing IIS/SharePoint exploitation, (2) China Chopper .aspx webshell on the same web tier, and (3) WinRM (T1021.006) — not WMI or RDP — for internal pivot. Joining w3wp.exe child-process recon to a WinRM pivot on the same host within a 6-hour window mirrors the BRONZE UNION/Iron Tiger intrusion timeline reported by S
